redemption.plainSummary: Your points are worth the most on flights, hotels, Apple or Tanishq via SmartBuy (₹1 each). With a big balance, transferring to airline partners can be worth even more — but confirm seats first. Avoid cashback (₹0.30) and generic vouchers (₹0.50).

methods.note: 1:1 to KrisFlyer, Flying Blue, Finnair; 2:1 to Accor. Best on premium-cabin awards — value varies by route, confirm the award is available before you transfer.
Half the value of travel. Use only if you can't use travel.

redemption.caps: Up to 2 lakh points per cycle; 1.5 lakh/month for flights, hotels, airmiles.

redemption.plainSummary: Redeem through SmartBuy. Best value is the Gold Catalogue (₹0.65 each), then flights and hotels (₹0.50). You can only use 50,000 points a cycle, so a big balance is spread over months.

methods.note: Up to 0.5 miles per point; real value varies by airline — usually weaker than the catalogue.

redemption.caps: Each path capped at 50,000 points per statement cycle.

redemption.plainSummary: Simplest as statement cashback — 1 CashPoint = ₹1 off your bill. But you can only redeem ₹3,000 worth a month, so a large balance clears over several months.

methods.note: ₹1 each, but capped at 3,000 a month.
Also possible; statement cashback is the easy default.

redemption.caps: Statement-cashback redemption capped at 3,000 CashPoints per month.

redemption.plainSummary: Nothing to redeem — cashback is automatic. It credits straight to your bill, so there are no points to collect, convert, or lose value on.

methods.note: Auto-credits to your bill. No action needed.

redemption.plainSummary: Your points are worth ₹0.25 each through FIRST Rewards. There's a ₹99 + GST fee per redemption, so redeem in larger batches — frequent small redemptions waste value.

methods.note: ₹0.25 per point. Redeem in big batches — the ₹99+GST fee hurts small redemptions.

redemption.caps: A ₹99 + GST fee applies per redemption.

redemption.plainSummary: Your points are worth ₹0.25 each through FIRST Rewards. There's a ₹99 + GST fee per redemption, so redeem in larger batches — frequent small redemptions waste value.

methods.note: ₹0.25 per point. Redeem in big batches — the ₹99+GST fee hurts small redemptions.

redemption.caps: A ₹99 + GST fee applies per redemption.

redemption.plainSummary: Your points are worth ₹0.25 each through FIRST Rewards. There's a ₹99 + GST fee per redemption, so redeem in larger batches — frequent small redemptions waste value.

methods.note: ₹0.25 per point. Redeem in big batches — the ₹99+GST fee hurts small redemptions.

redemption.caps: A ₹99 + GST fee applies per redemption.

redemption.plainSummary: Your points are worth ₹0.25 each through FIRST Rewards. There's a ₹99 + GST fee per redemption, so redeem in larger batches — frequent small redemptions waste value.

methods.note: ₹0.25 per point. Redeem in big batches — the ₹99+GST fee hurts small redemptions.

redemption.caps: A ₹99 + GST fee applies per redemption.

redemption.plainSummary: Your points are worth ₹0.25 each. A flat, modest value — redeem when convenient, no big difference between options.
Your points are worth up to ₹0.50 each on travel bookings, but only ₹0.25 elsewhere. Save them for travel to get the most.

methods.note: Up to ₹0.50 per point — the best use.
₹0.25 per point — save points for travel instead.

redemption.plainSummary: Nothing to redeem — cashback is automatic and credited to your statement.
Nothing to redeem — Amazon Pay cashback credits automatically to your Amazon Pay balance / statement.
Nothing to redeem — Swiggy cashback applies automatically as Swiggy Money.
Nothing to redeem — cashback is auto-credited to your statement within two days of your bill, no points or portals.

methods.note: Auto-credited within ~2 days of statement. No action needed.

redemption.plainSummary: Your rewards become Scapia Coins, worth ₹0.20 each (5 coins = ₹1). Best used inside the Scapia app for travel bookings.

methods.note: 5 coins = ₹1. Use within the Scapia app.

redemption.plainSummary: Your CashPoints are worth more on SmartBuy (₹0.25 each) than as statement cashback (₹0.20). Redeem via SmartBuy and don't let them sit — they expire after 24 months.
Your CashPoints are worth more on SmartBuy (₹0.25 each) than as statement cashback (₹0.20). Redeem via SmartBuy and don't let them sit — they expire after 24 months.
Redeem as statement cashback — it avoids the ₹99 + tax fee charged on catalogue redemptions. Accumulate before redeeming.

methods.note: Same value but a ₹99+tax fee applies — avoid.

redemption.caps: Each rewards bucket capped at 500 CashPoints/month.

redemption.plainSummary: Route redemptions through SmartBuy for ₹0.50 per point on flights and hotels. Never redeem as cashback (₹0.20) — it destroys value. Build points for travel instead.
Best value comes from SmartBuy travel (~₹0.50/pt) or transferring to airline/hotel partners. Cashback is poor value — avoid it.

methods.note: Up to ₹0.50 per point via SmartBuy.
1:1 to KrisFlyer, Flying Blue, Finnair; 2:1 Accor. Premium-cabin value varies — confirm the award first.

redemption.plainSummary: Your points are worth ₹0.25 each. Redeem through the SBI rewards catalogue or as vouchers — value is the same across options, so redeem when convenient.
Your points are worth ₹0.25 each. Redeem through the SBI rewards catalogue or as vouchers — value is the same across options, so redeem when convenient.
Your points are worth ₹0.25 each. The card's real value is the 10X on partner brands and GyFTR vouchers — redemption value itself is modest.
Your points are worth ₹0.25 each whichever way you redeem. It's a low value — this is a starter/backup card, not one to chase point value on.
Your points are worth ₹0.25 each. Modest value — use Coral for its movie, lounge and railway benefits rather than for point redemption.
Your points are worth ₹0.25 each, redeemed as statement credit or travel/shopping vouchers. Same value across options.
Use your points for flights, hotels and premium vouchers where they're worth ₹1 each. Avoid statement credit, where value drops to ₹0.40.

methods.note: 1:1 to Air India Maharaja Club — confirm award availability.

redemption.plainSummary: Your points are worth roughly ₹0.25 each through the YES rewards catalogue. Redeem for vouchers or products.
Your points are worth up to ₹0.25 each, best on flight and hotel redemptions. The 40,000 bonus is worth about ₹10,000 toward travel.

methods.note: Up to ₹0.25 per point on travel.

redemption.plainSummary: Your EDGE points are worth about ₹0.20 each in the EDGE rewards catalogue. The card's bigger value is partner app discounts, not point redemption.
Your EDGE points are worth about ₹0.20 each in the EDGE rewards catalogue. The card's bigger value is partner app discounts, not point redemption.
EDGE points here are weak (~₹0.20 each) and the earn rate is low. The real value is partner discounts — don't rely on point redemption.

methods.note: ~₹0.20 per point. Forfeited 30 days after card closure.

redemption.caps: Unredeemed points forfeited 30 days after card closure.

redemption.plainSummary: Your EDGE Miles are best transferred 1:1 to airline partners (Air India, Aeroplan) for travel. Renewal miles are worth about ₹1 each.

methods.note: 1:1 transfer — premium-cabin value varies, confirm the award first.

redemption.plainSummary: Use points for airline/hotel transfers and travel redemptions for the best value. Note: Magnus lost several transfer partners in 2024–26, so confirm current partners before relying on this.

methods.note: Value varies; partners reduced since 2024 — check what's live before transferring.

redemption.plainSummary: Your points are worth ₹1 each via Travel with Points, or transfer 1:1 to airline/hotel partners for premium travel — often even more on business-class seats.

methods.note: 1:1 to KrisFlyer, Flying Blue, Qatar, JAL, Accor, Marriott. Premium cabins can exceed ₹1/pt — confirm the award first.

redemption.plainSummary: Save your Membership Rewards for larger travel redemptions and the milestone vouchers (like the ₹10,000 Taj e-Gift at ₹7L spend). Statement credit dilutes value.

methods.note: Best on travel and milestone vouchers.

redemption.plainSummary: Save Membership Rewards for hotel transfers (Marriott Bonvoy ~₹0.50–₹1+) and premium air miles (~₹0.60–₹1+). Taj vouchers ~₹0.40. Statement credit is the weakest.

methods.note: Marriott Bonvoy 1:1; premium air miles. Value varies — confirm first.

redemption.plainSummary: Best used as instant fuel redemption at BPCL outlets — your points directly cut your fuel bill. Worth about ₹0.25 each.

methods.note: ₹0.25 per point, straight off your fuel bill.

redemption.caps: Accelerated categories capped at 7,500 Reward Points/month.

redemption.plainSummary: Points can transfer to Accor hotels (2 points = 1 Accor point) or go toward the rewards catalogue. Standard redemption value is modest — this card is better for its travel and lifestyle perks than for point value.

methods.note: 2 points = 1 Accor point. Value depends on the hotel booking.

redemption.plainSummary: Your points are worth about ₹0.25 each, whichever way you use them — statement credit or travel/shopping vouchers. Low per-point value, so the card's real value is its welcome vouchers and category use, not point redemption.

methods.note: Also ₹0.25 — no better than statement credit.

redemption.caps: Milestone bonus points capped at 20,000 per year.
